name: loadVideo
signature: loadVideo(QString filename, QString id, float x, float y, bool show, float alpha)
description: Loads a video file from disk and associates it with a unique identifier. Supported formats depend on the system's multimedia backend and installed codecs (MP4, WMV, MOV, AVI, etc.).

parameters.name: filename
parameters.description: Path to the video file. An absolute path is recommended.

parameters.name: x
parameters.description: X-coordinate from the left edge of the screen. If >1, it's absolute pixels; if between 0 and 1, it's relative to screen width.

parameters.name: y
parameters.description: Y-coordinate from the top edge of the screen. If >1, it's absolute pixels; if between 0 and 1, it's relative to screen height.

parameters.name: show
parameters.description: If `true`, the video starts playing immediately. If `false`, it loads but does not play.

parameters.name: alpha
parameters.description: Opacity of the video (0.0 = fully transparent, 1.0 = fully opaque).

compatibility.notes: ⚠️ Requires Qt Multimedia module (`ENABLE_MEDIA`). If Stellarium was built without media support, these functions will have no effect. Video format support varies by platform.

name: playVideo
description: Plays a previously loaded video. Can optionally keep the last frame visible after playback ends.

parameters.name: keepVisibleAtEnd
parameters.description: If `true`, the video pauses at the last frame instead of hiding the video window.

name: playVideoPopout
signature: playVideoPopout(QString id, float fromX, float fromY, float atCenterX, float atCenterY, float finalSizeX, float finalSizeY, float popupDuration, bool frozenInTransition)
description: Plays a video with a 'popout' effect: it appears to grow from a starting point (`fromX`, `fromY`), expands to a target center and size, and then shrinks back. Useful for interactive presentations and guided tours.

parameters.name: fromX
parameters.description: Start X position. If >1, absolute pixels; if between 0 and 1, relative to screen width.

parameters.name: fromY
parameters.description: Start Y position. If >1, absolute pixels; if between 0 and 1, relative to screen height.

parameters.name: atCenterX
parameters.description: Target center X position. If >1, absolute pixels; if between 0 and 1, relative to screen width.

parameters.name: atCenterY
parameters.description: Target center Y position. If >1, absolute pixels; if between 0 and 1, relative to screen height.

parameters.name: finalSizeX
parameters.description: Final width. `-1` = auto from aspect ratio, `>1` = absolute pixels, `0-1` = relative to screen width.

parameters.name: finalSizeY
parameters.description: Final height. `-1` = auto from aspect ratio, `>1` = absolute pixels, `0-1` = relative to screen height.

parameters.name: popupDuration
parameters.description: Duration of the grow (start) and shrink (end) transitions in seconds.

parameters.name: frozenInTransition
parameters.description: If `true`, the video pauses during the growing/shrinking transitions; if `false`, it plays while animating.

compatibility.notes: ⚠️ Requires Qt Multimedia module (`ENABLE_MEDIA`). This is an advanced feature.

name: pauseVideo
description: Pauses video playback. The video remains visible on screen and can be resumed with `playVideo()`.

name: stopVideo
description: Stops video playback completely and hides the video window. The video will restart from the beginning when `playVideo()` is called.

name: dropVideo
description: Unloads a video from memory and releases all associated resources. The video can no longer be played unless loaded again with `loadVideo()`.
example:
// Unload the video to free memory
StelVideoMgr.dropVideo("intro");

name: seekVideo
signature: seekVideo(QString id, qint64 milliseconds, bool pause)
description: Jumps to a specific position in the video. Can optionally pause after seeking. Useful for skipping to key scenes.

parameters.name: milliseconds
parameters.description: Position in milliseconds from the start of the video.

parameters.name: pause
parameters.description: If `true`, pauses the video after seeking. If `false`, it continues playing.

compatibility.notes: ⚠️ Requires Qt Multimedia module (`ENABLE_MEDIA`). May not work with all codecs or platforms (e.g., WMV on Windows).

name: setVideoXY
signature: setVideoXY(QString id, float x, float y, bool relative)
description: Moves the video to a new position on the screen. Can use absolute pixel coordinates or relative offsets.

parameters.name: x
parameters.description: X position or offset. If `relative` is false, behaves like `loadVideo()`; if true, it adds to the current X position.

parameters.name: y
parameters.description: Y position or offset. If `relative` is false, behaves like `loadVideo()`; if true, it adds to the current Y position.

parameters.name: relative
parameters.description: If `true`, the `x` and `y` values are added to the current position. If `false`, they are set as absolute positions.

name: setVideoAlpha
description: Sets the opacity (transparency) of the video. `0` = fully transparent, `1` = fully opaque.

compatibility.notes: ⚠️ Requires Qt Multimedia module (`ENABLE_MEDIA`). **Note:** On Qt5, only `0` (transparent) and `1` (opaque) may work. Qt6 supports semi-transparency well.

name: resizeVideo
signature: resizeVideo(QString id, float width, float height)
description: Changes the size of the video frame. Supports absolute pixels, relative to screen, or auto-scaling based on aspect ratio.

parameters.name: width
parameters.description: New width. `-1` = auto from aspect ratio, `>1` = absolute pixels, `0-1` = relative to screen width.

parameters.name: height
parameters.description: New height. `-1` = auto from aspect ratio, `>1` = absolute pixels, `0-1` = relative to screen height.

compatibility.notes: ⚠️ Requires Qt Multimedia module (`ENABLE_MEDIA`). Auto-scaling preserves the video's aspect ratio.

name: showVideo
description: Shows or hides the video window without unloading it. Hidden videos are still loaded and can be shown again instantly.

parameters.name: visible
parameters.description: `true` to show the video window, `false` to hide it.

name: getVideoDuration
description: Returns the total duration of the video in milliseconds. May return `-1` if the video has not been fully loaded or analyzed yet.
returnDescription: Duration in milliseconds, or `-1` if the duration is not yet known or an error occurred.

compatibility.notes: ⚠️ Requires Qt Multimedia module (`ENABLE_MEDIA`). Duration may only be available after playback has started (`playVideo()` or `pauseVideo()`).

name: getVideoPosition
description: Returns the current playback position of the video in milliseconds.
returnDescription: Current position in milliseconds, or `-1` if an error occurred.

name: getVideoResolution
description: Returns the native resolution of the video in pixels. May return an invalid `QSize` (`.isValid() == false`) if the video has not been fully loaded.
returnDescription: Video resolution as `QSize(width, height)`, or an invalid size if not yet known.

compatibility.notes: ⚠️ Requires Qt Multimedia module (`ENABLE_MEDIA`). Resolution may only be available after playback has started.

name: getVideoWidth
description: Returns the native width of the video in pixels. Returns `-1` if the video has not been fully loaded.
returnDescription: Video width in pixels, or `-1` on error.

name: getVideoHeight
description: Returns the native height of the video in pixels. Returns `-1` if the video has not been fully loaded.
returnDescription: Video height in pixels, or `-1` on error.
example:
var height = StelVideoMgr.getVideoHeight("intro");
core.debug("Video height: " + height);

name: muteVideo
description: Mutes or unmutes the audio track of the video.

compatibility.notes: ⚠️ Requires Qt Multimedia module (`ENABLE_MEDIA`). Only effective if audio is enabled and the video has an audio track.

name: setVideoVolume
description: Sets the audio volume level of the video. Values outside the 0-100 range are clamped.

compatibility.notes: ⚠️ Requires Qt Multimedia module (`ENABLE_MEDIA`). Only effective if audio is enabled.

name: getVideoVolume
description: Returns the current audio volume level of the video (0-100). Returns `-1` if the video is not found or audio is disabled.

name: isVideoPlaying
description: Returns whether a video is currently playing.
returnDescription: `true` if the video is playing, `false` if it is paused, stopped, or not loaded.
